meaning: adj.恐怖的,令人毛骨悚然的,以死亡为主题的
examples: Italian artist Bolla has transformed them into something a bit more sinister—macabre tokens of death that include an AK-47 assault rifle and human remains.
Most objects are violent, raw, macabre, and deliberately so.
But before macabre meant macabre why call the dance of death macabre?
His aesthetic is that of darkness and beauty, mixing two such polar elements with macabre elegance.
While the macabre aspect of his scheme offends many, it did not make Caramadre squeamish.
